methods.description: Analysis of core SCW03 to identify and characterize sedimentary facies based on color, fossil content, and sedimentary structure, and interpret the depositional environment and events.

steps.description: The core SCW03 mostly consists of greenish-grey silty clay and can be divided distinguished into three sedimentary facies according to color, fossils content, and sedimentary structure (Figure 2A,C).
steps.searchable_summary: Observe core sample and divide into sedimentary facies based on visual criteria
outputs: Defined sedimentary facies (A, B, C) within the core

steps.description: Facies A is characterized by yellowish-brown mottling structures. Shell fragments are not observed in this facies. In Facies B, the yellowish-brown mottling structures are less abundant and shell fragments are observed to occur sporadically. The size of shell fragments are about 2 mm in diameter. Facies C is represented by highly concentrated shells and shell fragments in several-centimeters-thick intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: Detail characteristics for each identified sedimentary facies
outputs: Detailed descriptions of facies A, B, and C (color, mottling, shell fragments)

steps.description: The sediments of core SCW03 are interpreted as deposited in a tidal flat [44,45]. It is very similar to the current tidal flat environment. Abundant mottling structures in facies A originate from an oxygen-rich environment, indicating that the sea level has gradually decreased slightly in facies B. In the meantime, highly concentrated shells and shell fragments of facies C are interpreted as sedimentation caused by flooding or storm events [45].
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret depositional environment and events from facies characteristics
inputs: Detailed descriptions of facies A, B, and C
outputs: Interpretation of depositional environment (tidal flat) and events (sea level changes, flooding/storm)
